user_query:
I’m polishing a couple of email drafts and need a tidy, down-to-earth version of each, plus a quick status note for my chair. Please work within the local workspace and deliver the following:

1) Rewrite existing drafts
- Inspect input/drafts/ and process every .txt email draft you find.
- Keep the first two lines (the headers that start with "To:" and "Subject:") exactly as they are in each file.
- Rewrite only the body into a warm, colloquial-but-professional tone that preserves every specific date, time, room number, course titles, and any URLs verbatim. Do not alter those details.
- Add a brief friendly sign‑off consistent with my voice (e.g., "See y’all soon,") followed by my name.
- Save the rewritten emails to output/drafts_rewritten/ using the same filename as the original (create the directory if needed).

2) Compose a weekly outreach status
- Using input/outreach_plan.json, compose one email-like status note to my department chair at output/weekly_update_to_chair.txt.
- Consider a plan item "Drafted" if there exists a file in input/drafts/ whose Subject line contains the item’s subject_keyword (case-insensitive). Otherwise mark it "Missing".
- Write a short opening in my colloquial voice, then include two sections: "Summary" (totals: number of plan items, how many drafted, how many missing) and "Details" (one line per item with: id, audience, subject_keyword, status, and the matched draft filename if applicable).

3) Create a structured inventory CSV
- Write output/draft_inventory.csv with one row per original draft in input/drafts/.
- Columns (header row required): filename,to,subject,dates_original,dates_revised,urls_original,urls_revised,word_count_original,word_count_revised,rewritten_path
- Extraction rules:
  • to: text after "To:" on line 1; subject: text after "Subject:" on line 2.
  • dates_*: list month–day tokens found in the body (e.g., "Oct 18") as they appear, separated by semicolons if multiple; leave empty if none.
  • urls_*: list any http(s) links exactly as they appear, semicolon‑separated if multiple; leave empty if none.
  • word counts: simple whitespace-split word count over the body only (exclude the two header lines).
  • rewritten_path: the path to the corresponding file in output/drafts_rewritten/.

Input paths:
- input/drafts/ (contains the .txt drafts)
- input/outreach_plan.json

Output paths:
- output/drafts_rewritten/ (rewritten drafts)
- output/weekly_update_to_chair.txt (status email)
- output/draft_inventory.csv (structured inventory)

Please keep this grounded in the provided files only and do not send any real messages. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
